name: smallstack-skills
description: AI skill index for Django SmallStack. Read the relevant skill before modifying the corresponding part of the codebase.

skills.name: Django Apps & CRUDView
skills.description: Creating new Django apps, CRUDView configuration, django-tables2 integration, title bar pattern. See also: api skill for REST API and auth management.
skills.use_when: Creating a new app, adding a model, building management pages, or wiring up CRUDView.

skills.name: Template System
skills.description: Template inheritance, available blocks (title, content, extra_css, extra_js, breadcrumbs), includes, and the thin-wrapper pattern for merge-safe customization.
skills.use_when: Creating or modifying HTML templates, understanding block inheritance, or adding includes.

skills.name: Building Themed Pages
skills.description: Recipe guide for building pages on the SmallStack theme. Covers the color-mix scale (4%-30%), page headers, tables, cards, stat widgets, badges, modals, and the CSS variable cheat sheet. Pages built this way work in all 10 theme/palette combinations with zero dark mode overrides.
skills.use_when: Building any new page that extends smallstack/base.html. This is the primary styling reference.

skills.name: Theming System Reference
skills.description: Complete CSS variable definitions for light/dark modes, color palette system (django, high-contrast, dark-blue, orange, purple), palette precedence, JavaScript API, dark mode persistence contract, and responsive breakpoints.
skills.use_when: Understanding the theming architecture, adding a new palette, or debugging theme/palette issues.

skills.name: Adding a Custom Theme
skills.description: Step-by-step tutorial for adding a custom CSS framework (Bootstrap, Tailwind, Tabler) alongside SmallStack's built-in theme. Covers the required template pieces (blocking script, SMALLSTACK config, theme.js), CSS override file, navbar partial, and static asset placement.
skills.use_when: The user wants to use their own CSS framework for public pages while keeping SmallStack's theme for admin tools.

skills.name: Theme Integration Scenarios
skills.description: Decision tree and step-by-step for three scenarios: (A) public site with no user login, (B) public site with user login, (C) build on SmallStack's theme. Includes settings, file placement, and common mistakes.
skills.use_when: The user is deciding how to integrate their theme, or an AI needs to understand which approach to take.

skills.name: Authentication
skills.description: Custom User model (settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL), auth views, LoginRequiredMixin, signup control (SMALLSTACK_SIGNUP_ENABLED), login/logout URLs, and protecting views.
skills.use_when: Working with auth views, protecting pages, configuring signup, or extending the User model.

skills.name: htmx Patterns
skills.description: htmx setup (vendored, no CDN), CSRF handling via hx-headers, partial templates, dual-response views (full page vs htmx partial), OOB swaps, and inline form submissions.
skills.use_when: Adding htmx interactions, building partials, or implementing inline updates.

skills.name: Help & Documentation System
skills.description: File-based help system with markdown rendering, _config.yaml structure, sections, categories, variables, FAQ pages, app-contributed docs, and search index.
skills.use_when: Adding or editing help pages, creating new doc sections, or configuring the help system.

skills.name: Settings & Configuration
skills.description: Split settings (base/development/production/test), environment variables via python-decouple, BRAND_* settings, SMALLSTACK_* feature flags, and .env file usage.
skills.use_when: Changing settings, adding environment variables, or understanding the split settings architecture.

skills.name: Timezone Handling
skills.description: TimezoneMiddleware, per-user timezone via UserProfile, localtime_tooltip template tag, and the CSS-only timezone tooltip.
skills.use_when: Working with dates/times, adding timezone-aware displays, or understanding the timezone middleware.

skills.name: Background Tasks
skills.description: Django Tasks framework with django-tasks-db backend. Task definition, enqueuing, the db_worker management command, and scheduled/periodic tasks.
skills.use_when: Adding background jobs, scheduled tasks, or understanding the task queue.

skills.name: Activity Tracking
skills.description: HTTP request logging middleware, RequestLog model, auto-pruning (configurable row cap), excluded paths, and the staff-only activity dashboard.
skills.use_when: Configuring request logging, excluding paths, or working with the activity dashboard.

skills.name: Logging & Audit Trail
skills.description: Django logging configuration, log levels, audit trail patterns, and structured logging.
skills.use_when: Configuring logging, adding audit trails, or debugging log output.

skills.name: Screenshot Workflow
skills.description: Visual verification using shot-scraper CLI and the screenshot_auth management command for authenticated screenshots. Light/dark mode, element selectors, and CI usage.
skills.use_when: Taking screenshots to verify UI changes, especially in both light and dark modes.

skills.name: Docker Deployment
skills.description: Docker Compose setup, multi-service configuration (web, worker, cron), volumes, health checks, and environment variables for containerized deployment.
skills.use_when: Setting up Docker, modifying the Dockerfile, or configuring container services.

skills.name: Kamal Deployment
skills.description: Zero-downtime VPS deployment with Kamal. deploy.yml configuration, secrets, SSL, volumes, accessories, and common commands.
skills.use_when: Deploying to production, configuring Kamal, or troubleshooting deployment issues.

skills.name: Development Workflow
skills.description: Branching strategy, testing with pytest, coverage targets, commit style, and code quality with ruff.
skills.use_when: Understanding development conventions, running tests, or preparing commits.

skills.name: Release Process
skills.description: Semantic versioning, release checklist, version bumping in pyproject.toml and _config.yaml, GitHub releases.
skills.use_when: Preparing a release, bumping the version, or creating a GitHub release.

skills.name: REST API
skills.description: CRUDView JSON API layer with Bearer token auth, token types (login/manual), access levels (auth/staff/readonly), auth management endpoints (register, me, password, deactivate), FK expansion, date filtering, aggregation, and CORS.
skills.use_when: Building or consuming REST API endpoints, managing tokens, or configuring API authentication and access levels.

skills.name: Frontend Integration
skills.description: Using SmallStack as a headless API backend for React, Vue, Next.js, or Svelte frontends. Covers system tokens, user lifecycle (register, login, password change, deactivate), CORS setup, and token architecture.
skills.use_when: Setting up SmallStack as a backend API for a frontend framework, or understanding the system token + login token architecture.

skills.name: Upstream Integration
skills.description: Pulling upstream SmallStack changes into downstream projects, resolving merge conflicts, and deploying after integration.
skills.use_when: Merging upstream SmallStack updates into a downstream project like opshugger or smallstack_web.
